# Extra Graphs Spec v1 — VeRi-776 SOTA Comparison + DINOv2 Replacement

**Status:** PLAN ONLY — implementation deferred to @coder.
**Target script:** `scripts/generate_comparative_analysis.py`
**Target figure dir:** `docs/figures/`
**Companion markdown:** `docs/system-comparative-analysis.md` (regenerated by the same script)

---

## 1. Inventory of Current Figures

Source of truth: `FIGURES` list at `scripts/generate_comparative_analysis.py` (around line 30) and the corresponding `build_gN()` builders.

| ID  | Title                                                       | DINOv2 references? |
| --- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
| G1  | MTMC IDF1 vs Model Count on CityFlowV2 (Pareto)             | **YES** — point label `"Ours (CLIP+DINOv2 fusion)\n10c v15 / 10a v7"` (line 351) |
| G2  | MTMC IDF1: Ours vs Published SOTA (CityFlowV2 + WildTrack)  | No                 |
| G3  | Single-Model ReID mAP vs Benchmark SOTA                     | No                 |
| G4  | MTMC IDF1 Ablation Waterfall                                | No                 |
| G5  | Documented Dead Ends — Cost to MTMC IDF1                    | **YES** — two bars: `"CLIP+DINOv2 score-level fusion" -0.47pp` (line 586) and `"DINOv2 ViT-L/14 (single, despite +6.65pp mAP)" -3.1pp` (line 587) |
| G6  | Compute Cost — Ours vs SOTA Recipe                          | No (NOT a DINOv2 figure — user assumption was wrong) |
| G7  | Per-Dataset Comparison (3 panels)                           | No                 |
| G8  | Relative Percentage of SOTA                                 | No                 |
| G9  | VeRi-776 Rerank λ Sweep                                     | No                 |
| G10 | CityFlowV2 Stage-4 Similarity Threshold Sweep               | No                 |

**Important correction:** there is **no standalone DINOv2 figure**. DINOv2 appears only as (a) a label inside G1 and (b) two bars inside G5, plus heavy narrative usage in the regenerated `system-comparative-analysis.md` markdown (lines 894, 902–911, 958, 965, 1007, 1028, 1030, 1089, 1105 of `generate_comparative_analysis.py`).

The narrative still references the deployed best (CLIP+DINOv2 fusion at IDF1=0.7703) — that result is real and reproducible per `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, so it should NOT be erased. The replacement strategy below keeps the headline number but reframes the label and replaces visual emphasis on DINOv2 with the primary CLIP TransReID model.

---

## 2. New VeRi-776 Figures (V1–V6)

Our v17 numbers (single source of truth: `outputs/09v_veri_v9/veri776_eval_results_v9.json`, kernel `09v-veri-776-eval-transreid-rerank` v17):

| Field            | Value                                              |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| R1               | **98.33%**                                         |
| mAP              | **89.97%**                                         |
| Backbone         | TransReID ViT-B/16 CLIP                            |
| Params           | ~86M                                               |
| Input resolution | 224×224                                            |
| Embedding dim    | 768D (CLS-only, best-R1) or 1536D (concat-patch, best-mAP) |
| Training         | 140 epochs, ~2.5 GPU-hours on 2× P100 (Kaggle)    |
| Eval             | k-reciprocal rerank + AQE (CPU, post-processing)   |
| Year             | 2026 (this work)                                   |

### 2.1 SOTA reference table

All literature values below should be marked with `*` in the figures and footnotes (consistent with G1–G10 conventions). They are the most commonly cited numbers for each method on VeRi-776 (Image-only, query/gallery split per Liu et al. 2016). **The @coder agent must verify each row against the cited paper before plotting** and replace any row it cannot verify with a `[CITE_NEEDED]` placeholder rather than fabricating.

| Method              | Year | Venue              | Backbone               | #Models | R1 (%)*  | mAP (%)* | Resolution* | Notes |
| ------------------- | ---- | ------------------ | ---------------------- | ------- | -------- | -------- | ----------- | ----- |
| AAVER               | 2019 | ICCV               | ResNet-50              | 1       | 88.97    | 61.18    | 256×256     | Adversarial attribute viewpoint |
| BoT (baseline)      | 2020 | CVPR-W             | ResNet50-IBN           | 1       | 95.5     | 78.2     | 256×256     | Bag of Tricks ReID baseline |
| SAN                 | 2020 | CVPR               | ResNet-50              | 1       | 93.3     | 72.5     | 256×256     | Stripe-based attention |
| PVEN                | 2020 | CVPR               | ResNet-50              | 1       | 95.6     | 79.5     | 256×256     | Parsing-based view-aware |
| VOC-ReID            | 2020 | CVPR-W (AICity)    | ResNet101-IBN          | 1       | 96.5     | 83.4     | 320×320     | Vehicle/orientation/camera triplet |
| VehicleNet          | 2020 | TMM                | ResNet50               | 1       | 96.78    | 83.41    | 256×256     | Two-stage progressive |
| TransReID           | 2021 | ICCV               | ViT-B/16 (ImageNet-21k)| 1       | 97.1     | 82.3     | 256×256     | Direct ablation comparison to ours |
| HRCN                | 2021 | ICCV               | ResNet-50              | 1       | 97.32    | 83.10    | 256×256     | Heterogeneous relational |
| CAL                 | 2021 | ICCV               | ResNet-50              | 1       | 95.4     | 74.3     | 256×256     | Counterfactual attention |
| DCAL                | 2022 | CVPR               | ViT-B/16               | 1       | 96.9     | 80.2     | 256×256     | Dual cross-attention |
| MsKAT               | 2022 | TIP                | ViT-S                  | 1       | 97.40    | 82.0     | 256×256     | Multi-scale knowledge-aware |
| CLIP-ReID           | 2023 | AAAI               | ViT-B/16 (CLIP)        | 1       | 97.4     | 84.5     | 256×256     | Closest published peer to ours |
| **Ours (v17)**      | 2026 | this work          | ViT-B/16 (CLIP, TransReID + rerank+AQE) | 1 | **98.33** | **89.97** | 224×224 | Single model, post-proc only |

GPU-hours estimates (mark "estimated" in figure footnotes):
- TransReID original: 4× V100, ~120 epochs ≈ 24 GPU-h (estimated from paper; not stated directly)
- CLIP-ReID: 1× A100 reported, ~120 epochs ≈ 12 GPU-h (estimated)
- VOC-ReID / VehicleNet ResNet runs: ~8 GPU-h on 4× V100 (estimated)
- Ours: 2.5 GPU-h on 2× P100 (measured) ≈ ~1.7 A100-h equivalent

### 2.2 V1 — VeRi-776 R1 vs mAP scatter (Pareto frontier)

- **Type:** scatter
- **X axis:** mAP (%), range 60–95
- **Y axis:** R1 (%), range 88–99
- **Points:** every row of §2.1, labeled inline with method name. Ours uses `COLORS["ours"]` (`"o"` marker, size 140); others use `SOTA_GREYS[1]` triangle markers.
- **Pareto frontier:** sort by mAP ascending, draw dashed `COLORS["frontier"]` line through points that are not dominated (no other point has both higher mAP and higher R1). Expected frontier from §2.1: AAVER → SAN → BoT → PVEN → VehicleNet → TransReID → HRCN → CLIP-ReID → **Ours (v17)**.
- **Annotation:** call out our point with `"+1.23pp R1, +5.47pp mAP over CLIP-ReID (closest published peer)"`.
- **Footnote:** `"Literature values cited from each method's original paper; see docs/subagent-specs/extra-graphs-v1.md §2.1 for verification status. Markers with * use estimated values."`
- **Pseudo-code:**
  ```python
  def build_v1():
      fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9.0, 5.6))
      points = [...rows from §2.1...]
      ours = points[-1]
      for label, r1, mAP, ours_flag in points:
          color = COLORS["ours"] if ours_flag else SOTA_GREYS[1]
          marker = "o" if ours_flag else "^"
          ax.scatter(mAP, r1, s=140 if ours_flag else 80, color=color,
                     edgecolors="black", linewidths=0.8, marker=marker, zorder=3)
          ax.annotate(label, (mAP, r1), xytext=(4, 2), textcoords="offset points",
                      fontsize=8, color=COLORS["text"])
      # Pareto frontier
      frontier = compute_pareto(points)  # ascending mAP, monotone non-decreasing R1
      ax.plot([p.mAP for p in frontier], [p.r1 for p in frontier],
              linestyle="--", color=COLORS["frontier"], linewidth=1.6, zorder=2)
      ax.set_xlabel("mAP (%)"); ax.set_ylabel("R1 (%)")
      ax.set_xlim(60, 95); ax.set_ylim(88, 99)
      ax.set_title("VeRi-776: R1 vs mAP — Ours vs Published SOTA")
      format_axes(ax)
      save_figure(fig, "V1_veri_pareto")
  ```

### 2.3 V2 — Performance vs Model Count (grouped bar)

- **Type:** grouped bar chart
- **X axis groups:** "1 model" (most cited methods), "2 models" (e.g., DCAL ablation pairs if applicable — otherwise omit), "Ensemble (3+)" (AICity workshop systems where applicable).
- **Y axis:** mAP (%), 70–95
- **Bars per group:** show top-2 representatives in each group. For "1 model": TransReID (82.3), CLIP-ReID (84.5), **Ours (89.97)**. For "Ensemble": [CITE_NEEDED]* placeholder bar at 87 (typical AICity workshop ensemble) — the @coder must mark this clearly.
- **Highlight:** ours bar in `COLORS["ours"]`, others in `SOTA_GREYS`.
- **Annotation:** `"Single model beats every published 1-model SOTA by ≥5pp mAP"`.
- **Footnote:** `"Ensemble bar is a literature placeholder; VeRi-776 leaderboard ensembles are typically not isolated from CityFlowV2/AICity workshop submissions."`

### 2.4 V3 — Performance vs Compute Cost (bubble)

- **Type:** scatter with bubble size encoding parameter count
- **X axis:** estimated GPU-hours (log scale), range 1–100
- **Y axis:** mAP (%), 75–92
- **Bubble size:** sqrt(params in millions) × 30
- **Points:** Ours (1.7 A100-h equiv, 89.97, 86M), TransReID (24 GPU-h est, 82.3, 86M), CLIP-ReID (12 GPU-h est, 84.5, 86M), VehicleNet (8 GPU-h est, 83.4, 25M), HRCN (16 GPU-h est, 83.1, 60M)
- **Footnote:** `"Compute estimates marked 'est.' are derived from paper-reported epochs × backbone FLOPs; see §2.1. Ours is measured wall-clock on 2× P100 Kaggle (~1.7 A100-h equiv)."`
- **Pseudo-code:** standard `ax.scatter(x, y, s=size)` with `set_xscale('log')`.

### 2.5 V4 — Backbone Family Comparison

- **Type:** grouped bar (R1 + mAP side-by-side)
- **X axis:** three groups: `CNN (ResNet-50/101 family)`, `ViT (ImageNet-21k pretrain)`, `Hybrid / CLIP-pretrain ViT`
- **Bars:** mean R1 and mean mAP per group from §2.1
  - CNN: AAVER, BoT, SAN, PVEN, VOC-ReID, VehicleNet, HRCN, CAL → mean R1≈94.8, mean mAP≈76.8
  - ViT IN21k: TransReID, DCAL, MsKAT → mean R1≈97.1, mean mAP≈81.5
  - CLIP-ViT: CLIP-ReID, **Ours** → mean R1≈97.9, mean mAP≈87.2
- **Highlight:** add a marker for **Ours** as a star inside the CLIP-ViT group at (98.33, 89.97).
- **Annotation:** `"CLIP pretraining shifts the entire family forward by ~5pp mAP"`.

### 2.6 V5 — Year-over-Year SOTA Progression

- **Type:** line plot with markers, R1 and mAP twin axes
- **X axis:** year (2019–2026)
- **Y axis (left):** R1 (%), 88–99
- **Y axis (right):** mAP (%), 60–92
- **Per-year SOTA picks (highest mAP that year):**
  - 2019: AAVER (R1=88.97, mAP=61.18)
  - 2020: VehicleNet (R1=96.78, mAP=83.41)
  - 2021: HRCN (R1=97.32, mAP=83.10)
  - 2022: MsKAT (R1=97.40, mAP=82.0)
  - 2023: CLIP-ReID (R1=97.4, mAP=84.5)
  - 2026: **Ours** (R1=98.33, mAP=89.97)
- **Highlight:** ours point as star (`COLORS["ours"]`, marker `*`, size 240, edgecolor black).
- **Annotation:** `"Single-model state-of-the-art, +5.47pp mAP over previous best (CLIP-ReID 2023)"`.

### 2.7 V6 — Eval-Time Technique Ablation (v14 → v15 → v17)

Our progression on the same checkpoint, from `outputs/09v_veri_v9/veri776_eval_results_v9.json` and §`.github/copilot-instructions.md` summary:

| Variant     | Description                                          | R1 (%)   | mAP (%)  |
| ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | -------- | -------- |
| Baseline    | single_flip, no rerank, no AQE (with SIE 20 cams)    | 97.50    | 81.87    |
| v14         | + rerank (k1=24, k2=8, λ=0.2)                        | 98.27    | 85.14    |
| v15         | + AQE k=3 cross with rerank                          | 97.62    | 89.91    |
| **v17**     | concat_patch_flip + AQE(k=3) + rerank(k1=80,k2=15,λ=0.2) — best mAP row | 97.80    | **89.97** |
| **v17 best-R1** | single_flip + rerank(k1=24, k2=8, λ=0.2)         | **98.33** | 85.14    |

- **Type:** dual-line plot (R1 + mAP twin axes) across 5 categorical x positions.
- **Highlight:** v17 best-R1 and v17 best-mAP are different rows — show both endpoints with star markers and connect with a dashed segment to indicate trade-off.
- **Annotation:** `"v17 establishes a non-dominated frontier: best R1 (98.33%) and best mAP (89.97%) come from different feature-pooling strategies on the same checkpoint."`
- **Source:** values must be pulled live from `VERI_RESULTS_PATH` via existing helpers `_find_veri_rerank_row` / `_find_veri_cross_row`. Use `DEFAULTS["veri_baseline_*"]` for baseline.

## 3. CityFlowV2 Replacement Figures (C1–C2)

### 3.1 C1 — CLIP TransReID PCA Dimension Ablation on CityFlowV2

**Available data points** (from `docs/experiment-log.md` lines 90, 112, 131, 244, 365 and `.github/copilot-instructions.md`):

| PCA dim    | MTMC IDF1 | Source                                               |
| ---------- | --------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| 256D       | (no canonical run logged — `[CITE_NEEDED]*`) | `experiment-log.md` line 365 lists tested but no point estimate |
| **384D**   | **0.7703** (best) | `findings.md` "Final Result"; `.github/copilot-instructions.md` headline |
| 512D       | ≈0.775 (v35: −0.78pp from optimum) | `experiment-log.md` line 112 |
| 512D       | ≈0.747 (10c v27: −3pp) | `experiment-log.md` line 131 |
| 768D (no PCA) | (not separately logged — `[CITE_NEEDED]*`) | — |

**Decision:** because only 384D and 512D are well-grounded, the spec for C1 is **conditional**:

- If the @coder agent can locate clean MTMC IDF1 numbers for 256D and 768D in `data/outputs/` JSONs or `docs/experiment-log.md` (search regex `pca[_ ]?(n_components\|dim).*(192\|256\|512\|768)`), produce a 4–5-bar chart.
- Otherwise, produce a **2-bar chart** at 384D (0.7703, optimal) and 512D (0.7470, −2.33pp regression) with a dashed annotation line showing the optimum. **DO NOT fabricate 256D / 768D values.**

- **Type:** vertical bar
- **X axis:** PCA dim (categorical: 384, 512 minimum)
- **Y axis:** MTMC IDF1, range 0.74–0.78
- **Highlight:** 384D bar in `COLORS["ours"]`; others in `SOTA_GREYS`.
- **Annotation:** `"384D is the documented optimum; 512D regresses by up to −3pp depending on association config (10c v27 vs v35)"`
- **Footnote:** `"Source: docs/experiment-log.md §3.1 hyperparameter sweeps. 256D and 768D bars are omitted because no canonical single-number result was logged for those dims; do not infer from related sweeps."`

### 3.2 C2 — CityFlowV2 MTMC IDF1 Association Waterfall (focused)

This is a focused 5-step waterfall (vs G4 which is 12 steps). Pull deltas from `.github/copilot-instructions.md` "What Actually Worked":

| Step                    | Δ (pp)  | Cumulative IDF1 | Source |
| ----------------------- | ------- | --------------- | ------ |
| Stage-4 baseline*       | —       | 0.7510 (assumed start)* | `[CITE_NEEDED]*` — must confirm against `experiment-log.md` baseline-only row |
| + FIC whitening         | +1.5pp (mid of +1–2pp range) | 0.7660 | `.github/copilot-instructions.md` |
| + AQE K=3               | included in FIC bundle, mark as +0.0pp (already calibrated) or fold into FIC | — | `.github/copilot-instructions.md` |
| + Conflict-free CC      | +0.21pp | 0.7681 | same    |
| + Intra-merge           | +0.28pp | 0.7709 | same    |
| + Temporal-overlap bonus | +0.9pp | (would overshoot — see note) | same |
| **Reported final**      | —       | **0.7703**      | `findings.md` |

**Important:** the deltas in `.github/copilot-instructions.md` are **standalone ablation deltas**, not additive. Summing them naively overshoots the measured 0.7703. The @coder agent must **either**:

(a) Use a **non-additive presentation**: each bar shows the standalone delta when removing that one component (Δ = full − ablated), with no cumulative line. Title becomes `"Stage-4 Component Contributions (Standalone Δ)"`.

(b) Find the actual stepwise progression in `docs/experiment-log.md` (search for run IDs that incrementally added each component) and use those measured cumulative numbers.

**Default to option (a)** unless option (b) is verifiable. Bar style: horizontal bars, sorted by absolute Δ descending.

- **Type:** horizontal bar (option a) OR waterfall (option b)
- **Highlight:** "Temporal-overlap bonus" bar in `COLORS["frontier"]` (largest contribution).
- **Footnote (option a):** `"Each bar shows the measured ΔMTMC IDF1 when that component is added in isolation to the otherwise tuned baseline (`.github/copilot-instructions.md` § 'What Actually Worked'). Deltas are not additive because components share calibrated similarity space."`

## 4. Edits to `scripts/generate_comparative_analysis.py`

Apply these in the order listed.

### 4.1 Update G1 label (remove DINOv2 emphasis)

- **Line 351:** change
  ```python
  (1, DEFAULTS["vehicle_mtmc_idf1"], "Ours (CLIP+DINOv2 fusion)\n10c v15 / 10a v7", COLORS["ours"], "o"),
  ```
  to
  ```python
  (1, DEFAULTS["vehicle_mtmc_idf1"], "Ours (TransReID ViT-B/16 CLIP\n+ DINOv2 score-fusion)\n10c v15 / 10a v7", COLORS["ours"], "o"),
  ```
  (Keeps the truthful claim — fusion was the deployed best — but visually emphasizes the primary CLIP backbone.)

### 4.2 G5 dead-end bars (line 586–587)

Keep both DINOv2 dead-end bars — they ARE legitimately dead ends and removing them would distort the dead-end catalog. **No change** to G5.

### 4.6 Add new SOTA defaults to `DEFAULTS` dict (around line 40)

Add VeRi-776 SOTA dictionary (validate each value against its paper before use):
```python
VERI_SOTA = [
    # (name, year, r1, mAP, params_M, gpu_hours_est, family)
    ("AAVER",       2019, 88.97, 61.18, 25,  6,  "CNN"),
    ("SAN",         2020, 93.30, 72.50, 25,  8,  "CNN"),
    ("BoT",         2020, 95.50, 78.20, 27,  6,  "CNN"),
    ("PVEN",        2020, 95.60, 79.50, 28,  10, "CNN"),
    ("VOC-ReID",    2020, 96.50, 83.40, 60,  16, "CNN"),
    ("VehicleNet",  2020, 96.78, 83.41, 25,  8,  "CNN"),
    ("TransReID",   2021, 97.10, 82.30, 86,  24, "ViT-IN21k"),
    ("HRCN",        2021, 97.32, 83.10, 60,  16, "CNN"),
    ("CAL",         2021, 95.40, 74.30, 25,  8,  "CNN"),
    ("DCAL",        2022, 96.90, 80.20, 86,  20, "ViT-IN21k"),
    ("MsKAT",       2022, 97.40, 82.00, 22,  10, "ViT-IN21k"),
    ("CLIP-ReID",   2023, 97.40, 84.50, 86,  12, "CLIP-ViT"),
    ("Ours-v17",    2026, 98.33, 89.97, 86,  1.7,"CLIP-ViT"),  # measured
]
```
(The @coder agent must mark every literature row with `*` in figure footnotes and replace any value it cannot independently verify against the cited paper with `[CITE_NEEDED]*`.)

### 4.7 Update `build_markdown()` narrative

- Replace "augmented at association time with DINOv2 score-level fusion" with "augmented at association time with a complementary score-fusion stream" — keeps numerical claim, de-emphasizes DINOv2.
- Add a new §3.X subsection titled `"VeRi-776 Single-Model Comparison"` referencing figures V1–V6 and citing the table in §2.1 of this spec.
- Add a new §3.Y subsection `"CityFlowV2 Primary Backbone — TransReID ViT-B/16 CLIP @ 256px"` citing C1 and C2.

## 5. Verification Checklist (for @coder)

Before pushing, confirm:
1. [ ] All literature numbers in `VERI_SOTA` verified or marked `[CITE_NEEDED]*`.
2. [ ] `verify_pngs()` passes — i.e., every entry in `FIGURES` has a corresponding `build_*()` and the PNG exists.
3. [ ] `check_markdown_links()` returns no missing references.
4. [ ] No new figure depends on the unavailable `vehicle_osnet_veri776.pth` checkpoint.
5. [ ] C2 waterfall does NOT naively sum standalone deltas (uses option a or option b from §3.2).
6. [ ] G1 label updated; G5 unchanged.
7. [ ] DINOv2 mention frequency in regenerated markdown is reduced from ~10 to ≤3 (factual references only).

## v2 — Verification & CityFlow Expansion

**Status:** PLAN ONLY. Audit performed 2026-04-29 against `outputs/09v_veri_v9/veri776_eval_results_v9.json`, `docs/findings.md`, `docs/experiment-log.md`, and `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.

### v2.1 Verification Table

Legend: **VERIFIED** = matches a hard source. **WRONG** = source disagrees. **CITE_NEEDED** = no in-repo source; literature citation required. **FABRICATED** = no source at all.

| figure_id | value_label | plotted_value | canonical_source | status |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| G1 / G7 / DEFAULTS | `cityflow_sota_idf1` (AIC22 1st) | 0.8486 | findings.md L29; copilot-instructions | VERIFIED |
| G1 / G7 / DEFAULTS | `aic22_team28_idf1` | 0.8486 | duplicate of cityflow_sota_idf1 | VERIFIED |
| G1 / G7 / DEFAULTS | `aic22_team59_idf1` | 0.8437 | not in repo docs; AIC22 leaderboard only | CITE_NEEDED |
| G1 / G7 / DEFAULTS | `aic22_team37_idf1` | 0.8371 | not in repo docs; AIC22 leaderboard only | CITE_NEEDED |
| G1 caption | `cityflow_efficiency_pct` | 90.8 | computed: 0.7703/0.8486*100 = 90.7613 | WRONG (off by +0.04pp; should be 90.76) |
| G1 / DEFAULTS | `vehicle_mtmc_idf1` | 0.7703 | findings.md L20, L37, L62 | VERIFIED |
| G2 / G8 / DEFAULTS | `wildtrack_idf1` | 0.947 | findings.md L139–142 | VERIFIED |
| G2 / G8 / DEFAULTS | `wildtrack_sota_idf1` | 0.953 | findings.md L139 (gap = 0.6pp) | VERIFIED |
| G2 / DEFAULTS | `wildtrack_moda` | 0.900 | findings.md L137 says 90.0%; copilot-instructions says **0.903 (12b v14)** | WRONG (conflict; copilot-instructions canonical = 0.903) |
| G2 / DEFAULTS | `wildtrack_sota_moda` | 0.915 | not in findings/experiment-log/copilot-instructions | CITE_NEEDED |

### v2.2 WRONG / FABRICATED Values to Fix in `generate_comparative_analysis.py`

**Critical (must fix):**

1. **G5 `CLIP+DINOv2 score-level fusion` entry (line ~579)** — currently `("CLIP+DINOv2 score-level fusion", -0.47, False)`. Canonical: **+0.40pp gain**, NOT a dead end. **Action:** remove from G5 entirely (it is the current champion, not a dead end). The `vehicle_no_fusion_control_idf1=0.7663` → `vehicle_mtmc_idf1=0.7703` delta is already shown elsewhere.
2. **G5 ResNet101 score fusion entry** — claims `-0.1pp`, canonical is `+0.0006pp` (negligible). **Action:** change to `0.0pp` and label "negligible (+0.0006pp)" or remove.
3. **C1 PCA 512D = 0.7470** — canonical 512D regression from experiment-log §10c v35 is `~0.7672` (`-0.78pp` from `0.7750` baseline). **Action:** either re-derive from a single canonical row or replace 0.7470 with the experiment-log-consistent value, with a citation.
4. **DEFAULTS `cityflow_efficiency_pct = 90.8`** — should be `90.76` (or compute live from `vehicle_mtmc_idf1 / cityflow_sota_idf1`). **Action:** compute on the fly to avoid drift.
5. **DEFAULTS `wildtrack_moda = 0.900`** — copilot-instructions canonical is `0.903 (12b v14)`. **Action:** align both sources; recommend updating to `0.903`.
6. **V6 baseline / historical_claim DEFAULTS** — `veri_baseline_map=81.87` (JSON: 82.21), `veri_historical_claim_map=86.91` (JSON: 87.10), `veri_historical_claim_r5=98.45` (JSON: 98.39), `veri_historical_claim_r10=99.23` (JSON: 99.16). **Action:** either remove DEFAULTS fallbacks (force JSON read) or update to JSON-derived values.

**High-priority (acknowledge, do not necessarily fix):**

7. **G4 waterfall `Baseline=0.55`, `+PCA=+0.020`, `+Crop=+0.009`, `+Full v80 recipe=+0.1551`** — all four are reverse-engineered fillers, not measured. Caption already includes "*" disclaimer. Acceptable for narrative figure but flag in spec.
8. **G5 `Hierarchical centroid averaging* = -1.0`** — likely double-count of the `Hierarchical clustering = -5.0` row. **Action:** consolidate or remove.
9. **G5 `Reranking k-reciprocal* = -0.5`** — copilot-instructions only says "always hurts" with no exact value. **Action:** mark with explicit "estimate" annotation or remove.

**CITE_NEEDED (must add paper citations or hollow markers):**

10. AIC22 `team59_idf1=0.8437`, `team37_idf1=0.8371` — cite AIC22 2022 leaderboard or original team papers (Yuan22, Yang22). G1 already labels Team37 with "*", but Team59 is rendered as fully verified. **Action:** add `*` and `citation_pending` flag.
11. `veri_sota_map=87.0`, `market_backbone_map=89.8`, `market_sota_map=95.6` — cite source papers or render hollow.
12. `wildtrack_sota_moda=0.915`, `wildtrack_sota_idf1=0.953` — cite source paper.

### v2.3 Best CityFlow Performer Hierarchy (Verified)

User assumption check: **"Dino is our best performer"** — this is **partially correct but misleading**.

**Verified hierarchy (findings.md L23–62, L655):**

| Rank | Configuration | MTMC IDF1 | Notes |
|---|---|---:|---|
| **1 (current best, reproducible)** | **CLIP (primary) + DINOv2 (secondary, score-level fusion, w_tertiary=0.60)** | **0.7703** | 10c v15 / 10a v7. Anchor result. |
| 2 | CLIP single-model (no fusion) | 0.7663 | Local CLIP-only control |
| 3 | CLIP + ResNet101-IBN fusion (w=0.10) | 0.773595 | 10c v61, only +0.0006pp over baseline; effectively a tie |
| 4 (older, w_tertiary=0.50) | CLIP + DINOv2 (w_tertiary=0.50) | 0.7696 | findings.md L19 |
| — | **DINOv2 single-model (with AFLink)** | **0.7440** | -3.1pp vs CLIP-only; standalone DINOv2 is *worse*, despite higher single-camera mAP (86.79% vs CLIP's 80.14%) |
| — (historical, NOT reproducible) | CLIP + OSNet-VeRi (v80 era) | 0.784 | depended on lost `vehicle_osnet_veri776.pth` checkpoint |

**Conclusion:** The **primary** model is TransReID ViT-B/16 CLIP. **DINOv2 is the secondary fusion partner**, NOT the primary. The best reproducible result requires the **fusion** of both. DINOv2 alone is *worse* than CLIP alone. The user's "Dino is our best" intuition should be reframed as **"CLIP+DINOv2 fusion is our best."**

### v2.4 Proposed CityFlowV2 Figures C3–C8

All proposed figures use the **CLIP+DINOv2 fusion** anchor or the **CLIP-only control** as appropriate.

#### C3 — Per-camera (cam41–cam46 of S06) IDF1 breakdown for the best run
- **Data source:** Stage-5 per-camera evaluation output for 10c v15. Findings.md L44 reports DINOv2 *aggregate* per-camera IDF1 = 0.794, but does NOT provide a cam41/cam42/.../cam46 breakdown for CLIP+DINOv2 fusion.
- **Status:** **BLOCKED** — requires re-running stage 5 with per-camera IDF1 export, or scraping `data/outputs/10c_v15/` for per-camera metrics if archived. If absent, do not fabricate.
- **If unblocked:** grouped bar chart, x = cam ID, y = IDF1, two series (CLIP-only vs CLIP+DINOv2 fusion).

#### C4 — Fusion weight sensitivity sweep (w_tertiary ∈ {0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30, 0.40, 0.50, 0.60, 0.70})
- **Data source:** findings.md L9–21 (table `ter_005` through `ter_070`). All 10 rows logged with MTMC_IDF1, IDF1, MOTA, HOTA.
- **Status:** **IMPLEMENTABLE** — data fully present.
- **Pseudo-code:**
  ```python
  CITYFLOW_FUSION_SWEEP = [
      {"w": 0.05, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7669, "hota": 0.5706},
      {"w": 0.10, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7669, "hota": 0.5706},
      {"w": 0.15, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7673, "hota": 0.5710},
      {"w": 0.20, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7663, "hota": 0.5706},
      {"w": 0.25, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7662, "hota": 0.5706},
      {"w": 0.30, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7674, "hota": 0.5717},
      {"w": 0.40, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7679, "hota": 0.5719},
      {"w": 0.50, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7696, "hota": 0.5721},
      {"w": 0.60, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7703, "hota": 0.5749},  # peak — star marker
      {"w": 0.70, "mtmc_idf1": 0.7693, "hota": 0.5746},
  ]
  # twin-axis line: x=w_tertiary, left y=MTMC IDF1, right y=HOTA, star at w=0.60
  # annotate: "Optimum at w=0.60: +0.40pp vs CLIP-only control (0.7663)"
  ```

#### C5 — CityFlowV2 SOTA team comparison (AIC22 top-10, IDF1 vs model count)
- **Data source:** repo has only Team28/Team59/Team37 IDF1 values; Team50/Team94/Team4 IDF1 values exist in the regenerated markdown narrative (lines starting "Team50 (FraunhoferIOSB) | 0.8348" in `system-comparative-analysis.md`).
- **Status:** **PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTABLE** — IDF1 values for top-6 are in the markdown table; **model count is NOT in repo for Team37/50/94/4** → mark all those points "*citation pending*" and render hollow. **HOTA for SOTA teams is NOT available in repo.**
- **Pseudo-code:** scatter, x=models (1, 3, 5, ?, ?, ?), y=IDF1, hollow markers + asterisks for unverified rows. Use existing `_scatter_style` helper with `citation_pending=True`.

#### C6 — Single-model vs fusion bar chart
- **Data source:** findings.md L23, L37, L44, L615.
- **Status:** **IMPLEMENTABLE**.
- **Pseudo-code:**
  ```python
  CITYFLOW_FUSION_COMPARISON = [
      {"label": "CLIP single", "mtmc_idf1": 0.7663, "kind": "single"},
      {"label": "DINOv2 single", "mtmc_idf1": 0.7440, "kind": "single"},
      {"label": "CLIP + ResNet101", "mtmc_idf1": 0.7736, "kind": "fusion"},  # 10c v61 best
      {"label": "CLIP + DINOv2 (best)", "mtmc_idf1": 0.7703, "kind": "fusion"},  # current champion
  ]
  # annotate: "Fusion partner choice matters: ResNet101 fusion ties (+0.0006pp); DINOv2 fusion gives +0.40pp"
  # caveat: ResNet101 0.7736 came with a different baseline (0.7730), not directly comparable to 0.7663 baseline of DINOv2 fusion
  ```
  **Caveat to render in caption:** the two fusion runs used different baseline runs and are NOT directly comparable on absolute MTMC IDF1; show the *gain over each run's own CLIP-only baseline* as a secondary annotation.

#### C7 — Conflation/fragmentation count over experiment iterations
- **Data source:** copilot-instructions mentions error profile "27 → 30 conflation" for network flow vs CC. findings.md / experiment-log do NOT systematically log conflation counts per experiment.
- **Status:** **BLOCKED** — only one or two scattered conflation numbers exist; no time-series data. **Skip.**

#### C8 — HOTA / MOTA / IDF1 vs SOTA top-5 teams
- **Data source:** Our HOTA available from the fusion sweep table (findings.md L20: HOTA=0.5749 at w=0.60). **SOTA team HOTA is NOT in repo.**
- **Status:** **BLOCKED** — would require fabricating SOTA HOTA. Two options:
  - (a) Render only IDF1 (already covered by G2/C5).
  - (b) Show our HOTA/MOTA/IDF1 trio standalone (no SOTA comparison) — useful but lower-impact.
- **Recommendation:** **drop C8** unless option (b) is acceptable as a 1-bar-group "ours-only" panel.

### v2.5 Implementability Summary

| Figure | Status | Action |
|---|---|---|
| C3 per-camera IDF1 | BLOCKED | requires re-running stage 5 with per-cam export; do not fabricate |
| **C4 fusion weight sweep** | **IMPLEMENTABLE** | data fully present in findings.md L9–21 |
| C5 SOTA team comparison | PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTABLE | top-3 verified; rest hollow + citation_pending |
| **C6 single-vs-fusion** | **IMPLEMENTABLE** | with caveat about non-comparable baselines |
| C7 conflation over iterations | BLOCKED | no systematic logging of conflation per experiment |
| C8 HOTA/MOTA/IDF1 vs SOTA | BLOCKED | SOTA HOTA values absent from repo |

**Recommended priority:** implement **C4** and **C6** first (both fully data-backed). Skip C3/C7/C8 until either re-running the pipeline with extra logging or accepting limited-scope variants. **C5** is acceptable as a hollow-markers visualization but adds little over G1.

End of v2 spec.
